samples.xml_description:
Implement an unsigned 8bit multiplier based on pipelining processing. Clock. Active-low reset. Input enable bit for multiplication. Multiplicand. Multiplier. Output enable bit for multiplication. Product. MUL_WIDTH is the width of multiplier and multiplicand.

Reset the internal registers.

if (!rst_n) begin mul_en_out_reg <= 1'b0; mul_a_reg <= {MUL_WIDTH{1'b0}}; mul_b_reg <= {MUL_WIDTH{1'b0}}; end

Update the mul_en_out based on the mul_en_in.

//mul_en_out is the output enable bit, mul_en_in is the input enable bit always @(posedge clk) begin mul_en_out_reg[MUL_WIDTH-1:0] <= {MUL_WIDTH{mul_en_in}}; end

Update the mul_a_reg and mul_b_reg based on the mul_a and mul_b.

//mul_a_reg and mul_b_reg store the input multiplicand and multiplier always @(posedge clk) begin if (mul_en_in) begin mul_a_reg <= mul_a; mul_b_reg <= mul_b; end end

Generate the partial products based on the mul_b_reg.

//Generate partial products based on the multiplier bits wire [MUL_WIDTH-1:0] temp[0:MUL_WIDTH-1]; generate genvar i; for (i = 0; i < MUL_WIDTH; i = i + 1) begin: gen_partial_products assign temp[i] = mul_a_reg && {MUL_WIDTH{mul_b_reg[i]}}; end endgenerate

Calculate the partial sum based on the generated partial products.

//Calculate partial sums based on the generated partial products reg [2*MUL_WIDTH-1:0] sum[0:MUL_WIDTH-1]; generate genvar j; for (j = 0; j < MUL_WIDTH-1; j = j + 1) begin: add_partial_products always @(posedge clk) begin if (mul_en_in) begin sum[j+1] <= sum[j] + temp[j]; end end end endgenerate

Calculate the final product based on the last partial sum.

//Calculate the final product based on the last partial sum always @(posedge clk) begin if (mul_en_in) begin mul_out_reg <= sum[MUL_WIDTH-1] + temp[MUL_WIDTH-1]; end end

Assign the output based on the mul_en_out.

//Assign the output based on the mul_en_out always @(posedge clk) begin if (mul_en_out) begin mul_out <= mul_out_reg; end else begin mul_out <= 0; end end
